steps.title: Identify the critical value where standard deviations are equal
steps.content:
Compare the first five elements of each data set:
Data set A: 8, 11, 12, 15, 21, 27
Data set B: 9, 12, 13, 16, 22, h
Notice that B's first five elements are each exactly 1 more than A's: (9 = 8+1), (12 = 11+1), (13 = 12+1), (16 = 15+1), (22 = 21+1). If $h = 28$, then the pattern continues since $28 = 27 + 1$.

This means when $h = 28$, every value in B is exactly 1 more than the corresponding value in A. A fundamental property of standard deviation is that adding a constant to all data points shifts the mean by that constant but does NOT change the deviations or the standard deviation itself. Therefore: $$\text{SD}_B = \text{SD}_A \quad \text{when} \quad h = 28$$

steps.title: Test each candidate value to determine if $SD_A > SD_B$
steps.content:
For h = 23:
Sum = 95, Mean = 95/6, $\Sigma x^2 = 1663$
Variance = $\frac{1663}{6} - \left(\frac{95}{6}\right)^2 = \frac{953}{36}$
So $\text{SD}_B = \sqrt{\frac{953}{36}} \approx 5.14$

For data set A (already computed): Variance = $\frac{377}{9} = \frac{1508}{36}$, so $\text{SD}_A = \sqrt{\frac{377}{9}} \approx 6.47$

Since $\frac{953}{36} < \frac{1508}{36}$, we have $\text{SD}_A > \text{SD}_B$ ✓

For h = 28:
By the shift property from Step 1, $\text{SD}_B = \text{SD}_A$, so the strict inequality $\text{SD}_A > \text{SD}_B$ is NOT satisfied ✗

For h = 29:
Sum = 101, Mean = 101/6, $\Sigma x^2 = 1975$
Variance = $\frac{1975}{6} - \left(\frac{101}{6}\right)^2 = \frac{1649}{36}$
So $\text{SD}_B = \sqrt{\frac{1649}{36}} \approx 6.76$

Since $\frac{1649}{36} > \frac{1508}{36}$, we have $\text{SD}_A < \text{SD}_B$, so the condition is NOT satisfied ✗

steps.title: Determine which candidates satisfy the condition
steps.content:
From Step 2:
  • Option I ($h = 23$): Satisfies SD$_A$ > SD$_B$ ✓
  • Option II ($h = 28$): Does not satisfy (SD$_A$ = SD$_B$)
  • Option III ($h = 29$): Does not satisfy (SD$_A$ < SD$_B$)
Only Option I satisfies the required condition. That matches choice A — A is correct.
